cases.task: Create a ReaderLab package from a conceptual product specification.
cases.baseline_risk: May misroute product specs as engineering docs and over-clean the body.
with_skill_expected: routes conceptual product specs as book_or_longform
preserves full body in 10_中文精读/ single-page reader units
keeps body and companion notes in the same annotatable Markdown page
keeps any cleaning candidates in audit instead of silently removing text
forbidden: uses cleaned_body for conceptual product specs without explicit reason
drops product principles or boundaries as boilerplate
splits one reader unit into separate body, companion, and comment-question files

cases.task: Create a ReaderLab package from a longform source.
cases.baseline_risk: May summarize the source and omit the body track.
with_skill_expected: includes 10_中文精读/ with one annotatable Markdown page per reader unit
places source body and nearby AI companion notes in the same reader page
does not let AI companion text replace source body
ends with human review rather than automatic promotion
forbidden: claims public validation from private source
creates generated annotation-question files as the default comment surface

cases.task: Create a ReaderLab package from SKILL.md or engineering workflow material.
cases.baseline_risk: May either paste execution boilerplate as body or over-summarize away operational constraints.
with_skill_expected: includes cleaned body in the same reader page as nearby companion notes
preserves purpose, trigger, user intent, workflow, constraints, failure conditions, output requirements, and design highlights
forbidden: promotes host-specific runtime shell as ReaderLab architecture
forces readers to jump between body files and companion files for the same unit

cases.task: Create a ReaderLab package from a folder with body docs, templates, scripts, and examples.
cases.baseline_risk: May flatten the folder into one generic summary.
with_skill_expected: keeps scripts/templates distinct from primary body unless they are the material being read
forbidden: treats every file as equivalent body material
